title: Intent Predicate Algebra — Project Intelligence
description: Formal algebra of the six intent predicates (select_one_of, optional, phase_membership, cross_cutting_injection, blocks, invariant) that drives intent-sound orchestration (decompose/bound/gate). Every predicate has (1) a syntactic shape that constrains its declaration, and (2) a satisfaction semantics that is decidable by a jq pure-sub-language evaluator over a finite resolved-set JSON document. The 'invariant' predicate grammar is the only deliberately new shape; the others are reifications of fields that already exist in struct.json (phases.select_one_of, components.optional, etc.) with explicit per-predicate satisfaction rules.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bumped on grammar changes; the validator checks this.

predicates.description: Per-predicate definitions: syntax + satisfaction semantics + decidability argument.

SelectOneOf.description: Reification of phases[].select_one_of. SATISFACTION: in the resolved plan, |selected ∩ S| == 1 (exactly one branch is selected and the others are absent).

syntax.description: Where the predicate is declared in struct.json.

satisfaction.const: resolved.selections[phase_name] in S  AND  resolved.selected_components ∩ S == {resolved.selections[phase_name]}

decidability.const: O(|S|). Pure set-membership check on a finite set; always terminates.

OptionalPredicate.description: Reification of components[].optional (+ cross_cutting[].default_enabled). SATISFACTION: if disabled (optional + default_enabled absent or false), component absent from resolved set; if enabled, present.

satisfaction.const: if optional and not enabled then name not in resolved.selected_components else name in resolved.selected_components

decidability.const: O(1) per component. Lookup in a finite set.

PhaseMembership.description: Each resolved component must be reachable from an active phase (via core_components/optional_components/select_one_of/select_subset_of) OR via cross_cutting injection whose injects_into intersects active phases.

satisfaction.const: for every c in resolved.selected_components: exists phase p such that c in (p.core_components ∪ p.optional_components ∪ p.select_one_of ∪ p.select_subset_of) OR exists cross_cutting x such that c in x.components and x.injects_into ∩ active_phases != empty

decidability.const: O(N * (|P| + |X| * |X.components|)). Bounded nested-loop on finite sets.

CrossCuttingInjection.description: For each cross_cutting X, every phase p in X.injects_into must exist in the declared phases set.

decidability.const: O(|X| * |X.injects_into|). Subset check on finite sets.

Blocks.description: Each todo t appears in wave[t] where wave[t] > max(wave[b]) for every blocker b in t.blocks. Equivalently: no (b -> t) edge goes forward.

satisfaction.const: for every (t, b) with b in t.blocks: plan.wave[t] > plan.wave[b]

decidability.const: O(|T| * max_blocks_per_todo). Walk the wave map and check the strict-greater-than predicate.

Invariant.description: Declarative jq-predicate over a named scope of the resolved component set. The grammar is closed under the jq pure-sub-language and therefore decidable.

scope.description: How to materialise the collection. 'all_components' = full resolved set. 'components_in_phases' = per-phase bucket (predicate runs once per phase). 'components_in_phase:<name>' = single phase bucket. 'set:<name>' = a user-defined named set declared elsewhere.

quantifier.description: forall=every element must satisfy predicate; exists=at least one must.

predicate.description: A jq pure-sub-language boolean expression evaluated against each element (which is a component object: {name, exports, imports, optional, module, phase?, ...}). MUST be side-effect-free. The element is available as '.'.

evaluation.const: scope_materialise(plan) -> array; for each element e: ok = jq -e 'predicate(e)'; quantifier-fold(ok). Violation = ok=False with the element's name in the error report.

decidability.const: jq is total on finite inputs (jq evaluates a finite AST over a finite JSON document and always terminates). Each invariant is O(N) per predicate where N is the scope collection size. Iteration over a finite set of declared invariants is finite. Therefore the conjunction of all invariant checks is decidable.

examples.name: no_optional_in_core
examples.message: Optional components must not be in the resolved core set

examples.name: importer_declares_dependency
examples.message: Every import must be a non-empty string
